issues_found: Cross-objective and within-objective node connectivity failures: Objective 1 referenced NC_HW_sites and NC_PLACES as inputs without listing them as data sources; Objective 2 referenced outputs from Objective 1 (asthma_proximity.csv, nc_tract_centroids.gpkg) without declaring them as resolvable inputs.
Objective 1 Step 1 produced a single combined GeoPackage for two layers, creating ambiguity in downstream steps about which layer is being used.

Objective 1 Moran’s I / Gi* steps implicitly required an attribute join of asthma/proximity data to tract polygons but lacked an explicit join step, risking disconnected or non-reproducible workflows.
Objective 1 Step 7 redundantly used asthma_proximity.csv even though the comparison can be performed using a hotspot layer that already contains proximity fields (after proper joining).
Objective 2 Step 1 referenced vague covariate inclusion (“other available ratios”) instead of explicit, registry-verified column names.
Objective 2 feature engineering did not specify a zero-safe log transform for distance (log(0) risk).
changes_made: Performed cross-objective deduplication: kept centroid/proximity derivation in Objective 1 and updated Objective 2 to consume those outputs as declared inputs (as data_requirements entries referencing prior objective outputs).
Added missing Objective 1 data_requirements entries for NC_HW_sites and NC_PLACES to resolve source_id connectivity.
Split Objective 1 reprojection into two explicit outputs (projected tracts; projected hazardous-waste sites) to remove layer ambiguity.
Inserted an explicit attribute join step to create a tract GeoPackage containing Asthma_prev and proximity metrics before Moran’s I and Gi* computations.
Simplified Objective 1 hotspot-vs-nonhotspot proximity comparison to use the hotspot layer containing proximity fields, removing an unnecessary input.
Rewrote Objective 2 join step to use only explicit, registry-verified covariates and updated log transform to ln(nearest_hw_dist_m + 1).
Renumbered steps to maintain coherent pipelines and verified within-objective connectivity after edits.

objectives.title: Assess whether asthma prevalence is spatially clustered across North Carolina census tracts and whether hazardous-waste proximity aligns with high-asthma clusters
objectives.hypothesis: Census tracts with smaller nearest-site distance to hazardous waste facilities and/or larger hazardous-waste facility counts within 5000 meters have higher Asthma_prev and are overrepresented in statistically significant high-asthma clusters.

data_requirements.description: North Carolina census tract polygon boundaries with tract identifier GEOID.

data_preprocessing.rationale: Tract geometries require derived proximity metrics from hazardous waste point locations and an attribute join of tract-level asthma prevalence.

what_each_contributes.NC_tract: tract polygons and GEOID for spatial weights and mapping
what_each_contributes.NC_HW_sites: hazardous waste facility point geometries for distance and buffer counts
what_each_contributes.NC_PLACES: tract-level asthma prevalence column Asthma_prev keyed by GEOID

data_preprocessing.method: Reproject to a meter-based CRS; compute tract centroids; compute nearest distance and 5000 m counts to NC_HW_sites; join Asthma_prev by GEOID; attach metrics to tract polygons for spatial statistics.

data_requirements.description: Hazardous waste facility point locations for North Carolina with SITE_NAME.

data_preprocessing.rationale: Handled in the combined preprocessing workflow with NC_tract and NC_PLACES.

data_requirements.description: Tract-level health estimates with asthma prevalence column Asthma_prev and join key GEOID.

data_preprocessing.rationale: Joined by GEOID within the objective workflow.

steps.description: Reproject NC_tract to a common projected CRS in meters suitable for distance calculations while preserving GEOID.
steps.expected_output: Projected tract polygons with GEOID in a meter-based CRS.

steps.description: Reproject NC_HW_sites to the same projected CRS used for nc_tract_projected.gpkg while preserving SITE_NAME.
steps.expected_output: Projected hazardous waste facility points in the same meter-based CRS as the tracts.

steps.description: Compute tract centroids from nc_tract_projected.gpkg and retain GEOID for each centroid feature.
steps.expected_output: Tract centroid point features keyed by GEOID.

steps.description: For each GEOID, compute nearest_hw_dist_m as the Euclidean distance in meters from the tract centroid to the nearest hazardous waste facility point and compute hw_count_5km as the count of hazardous waste facility points within 5000 meters of the centroid.
steps.expected_output: A GEOID-keyed table with nearest_hw_dist_m and hw_count_5km.

steps.description: Left-join tract_proximity_metrics.csv to NC_PLACES on GEOID and retain GEOID, Asthma_prev, nearest_hw_dist_m, and hw_count_5km.
steps.expected_output: A GEOID-keyed table containing Asthma_prev and hazardous-waste proximity metrics.

steps.description: Join asthma_proximity.csv to nc_tract_projected.gpkg by GEOID to create tract polygons with attributes Asthma_prev, nearest_hw_dist_m, and hw_count_5km.
steps.expected_output: Tract polygons with Asthma_prev and proximity attributes suitable for spatial statistics.

steps.description: Compute Global Moran’s I for Asthma_prev using queen contiguity weights defined on tract polygons and permutation-based p-values.
steps.expected_output: Global Moran’s I results for Asthma_prev including statistic, z-score, and permutation p-value.

steps.description: Compute Getis-Ord Gi* for Asthma_prev using queen contiguity weights on tract polygons and write Gi* z-scores and p-value-based significance class per GEOID.
steps.expected_output: Tract polygons with Gi* z-scores and hotspot/coldspot significance classification for Asthma_prev.

steps.description: Using asthma_hotspots.gpkg, subset tracts to statistically significant hotspots for Asthma_prev (Gi* p < 0.05 and positive Gi* z-score) versus all other tracts, then compare nearest_hw_dist_m and hw_count_5km distributions between groups using Mann–Whitney U tests.
steps.expected_output: Group medians and Mann–Whitney U test statistics and p-values for nearest_hw_dist_m and hw_count_5km comparing hotspots vs non-hotspots.

objectives.title: Estimate how the hazardous-waste proximity–asthma association varies by socioeconomic and demographic context and whether the association is spatially non-stationary
objectives.hypothesis: After adjustment for tract context, the association between hazardous waste proximity and Asthma_prev is stronger in tracts with higher ratio_poverty, higher ratio_uninsured, and higher ratio_black, and the proximity effect varies across space.

data_requirements.description: Tract-level socioeconomic and demographic covariates keyed by GEOID, including ratio_poverty, ratio_uninsured, med_income, ratio_black, and ratio_hispanic.

data_preprocessing.rationale: Effect-modification modeling requires integrating Asthma_prev and proximity metrics with ACS covariates in a single GEOID-keyed table; spatially varying modeling also requires centroid coordinates.

what_each_contributes.NC_ACS_data: ratio_poverty, ratio_uninsured, med_income, ratio_black, ratio_hispanic keyed by GEOID
what_each_contributes.asthma_proximity.csv: Asthma_prev, nearest_hw_dist_m, hw_count_5km keyed by GEOID
what_each_contributes.nc_tract_centroids.gpkg: centroid geometry for extracting x and y coordinates keyed by GEOID

data_preprocessing.method: Left-join ACS covariates to asthma_proximity.csv by GEOID; filter to complete cases; create transformed/standardized predictors and specified interactions; attach centroid x/y coordinates by GEOID; fit interaction OLS and geographically weighted regression.

data_requirements.description: Objective 1 output table keyed by GEOID containing Asthma_prev, nearest_hw_dist_m, and hw_count_5km.

data_preprocessing.rationale: Consumed as an input from Objective 1 output.

data_requirements.description: Objective 1 output centroid layer keyed by GEOID for spatially varying coefficient estimation.

data_preprocessing.rationale: Consumed as an input from Objective 1 output.

steps.description: Left-join NC_ACS_data to asthma_proximity.csv on GEOID and retain GEOID, Asthma_prev, nearest_hw_dist_m, hw_count_5km, ratio_poverty, ratio_uninsured, med_income, ratio_black, and ratio_hispanic.
steps.expected_output: A GEOID-keyed modeling table containing outcome, exposures, and covariates.

steps.description: Filter tract_asthma_prox_acs.csv to rows with non-missing values for Asthma_prev, nearest_hw_dist_m, ratio_poverty, ratio_uninsured, med_income, ratio_black, and ratio_hispanic.

Create ln_nearest_hw_dist_m = ln(nearest_hw_dist_m + 1), create z_ratio_poverty, z_ratio_uninsured, z_med_income, z_ratio_black, and z_ratio_hispanic as z-scores of ratio_poverty, ratio_uninsured, med_income, ratio_black, and ratio_hispanic, and create interaction terms ln_nearest_hw_dist_m_x_z_ratio_poverty = ln_nearest_hw_dist_m * z_ratio_poverty and ln_nearest_hw_dist_m_x_z_ratio_black = ln_nearest_hw_dist_m * z_ratio_black.
steps.expected_output: A modeling-ready feature table including transformed exposure, standardized covariates, and specified interaction terms.

steps.description: Fit an ordinary least squares model with dependent variable Asthma_prev and predictors ln_nearest_hw_dist_m, z_ratio_poverty, z_ratio_uninsured, z_med_income, z_ratio_black, z_ratio_hispanic, ln_nearest_hw_dist_m_x_z_ratio_poverty, and ln_nearest_hw_dist_m_x_z_ratio_black, and report heteroskedasticity-robust (HC) standard errors.
steps.expected_output: OLS coefficient table and model diagnostics including robust standard errors and p-values.

steps.description: Join centroid coordinates from nc_tract_centroids.gpkg to tract_modeling_features.csv by GEOID and write numeric x and y coordinate columns named x and y.

Fit a geographically weighted regression using an adaptive bandwidth selected by minimizing AICc and a bisquare kernel with dependent variable Asthma_prev and predictors ln_nearest_hw_dist_m, z_ratio_poverty, z_ratio_uninsured, z_med_income, z_ratio_black, and z_ratio_hispanic, and output per-GEOID local coefficients and local R2.
steps.expected_output: Per-tract local coefficient estimates (including the ln_nearest_hw_dist_m coefficient) and local R2 for assessing spatial non-stationarity.
